summary:
Perfect! The test passes successfully. I've found and fixed the needle - the timing side-channel vulnerability in the authentication system.

The problem was in the `compareHashes` function in `/app/auth/compare.go`, which was using simple string equality (`==`) for comparing password hashes. This created a timing vulnerability where attackers could measure how long comparisons take to gradually determine the correct hash character by character.

I fixed it by replacing the vulnerable comparison with `crypto/subtle.ConstantTimeCompare`, which ensures that hash comparisons take the same amount of time regardless of how many characters match, eliminating the timing side-channel attack vector.
